source_pr.title: *: resort fields for reducing the memory allocated
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve? 
#13925

### What is changed and how it works?
resort the fields according golangci-lint maligned output

```
types/json/binary_test.go:159:16: struct of size 80 bytes could be of size 72 bytes (maligned)
	var tests = []struct {
	              ^
sessionctx/stmtctx/stmtctx.go:146:16: struct of size 24 bytes could be of size 16 bytes (maligned)
type StmtHints struct {


executor/distsql.go:202:26: struct of size 392 bytes could be of size 376 bytes (maligned)
type IndexReaderExecutor struct {
                         ^
executor/distsql.go:317:26: struct of size 472 bytes could be of size 432 bytes (maligned)
type IndexLookUpExecutor struct {
                         ^
table/index.go:33:19: struct of size 32 bytes could be of size 24 bytes (maligned)
type CreateIdxOpt struct {
                  ^
```

### Check List 


Release note

symptoms: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test. | tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
failure_signature: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test. | tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test. | tk.MustQuery("select sum(a) from t1 group by b order by b;").Check(testkit.Rows("<nil>", "3", "2", "2")) (executor/aggregate_test.go)
